item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that it had transformed into a fish, and that's why they never found it

Totally understandable! The fish does have a nifty new deity hat after all.

it's just been there ever since was pretty dang funny.

Glad you think so! Thanks for the nice comment! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this a lot.

I thought you might find it funny to know that midway through reading this, I thought that the fish itself was Osiris' penis; that it had transformed into a fish, and that's why they never found it. So finding out that the fish just ate his penis however many thousands of years ago and it's just been there ever since was pretty dang funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeyeye you know it, thanks for the reread

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Egyptian-Foundation family feud! Hooray!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Sons of Horus just really want their grandpa's property back, and they don't think asking nicely is the way to go about it d:

(thank you very much for the updoot)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks ihp, ancient deities clowning on the Foundation is something I think I'm into as an author lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that the Egyptian Pantheon forced the Foundation to translate "DICK" from Heiroglyphics is enough to get an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the nice comment! "Chaotic" was definitely the kind of energy we were going for here.

lvdoomien thought up the Sons of Horus graffiti bit—I felt like that just had to be included. Glad you enjoyed the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is insanely chaotic in a way I enjoyed. I found the depictions of the Egyptian pantheon of gods as common thugs doing graffiti to be hilarious. This was an easy upvote in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dang, that's a lot of hieroglyphs. Thanks for the chart! Pretty cool to see the full character block for all these symbols.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, it's in the egyptian hieroglyph character block. However, it doesn't render on Chrome on Windows 10, now that I check again. I guess the devs don't want people to see it. Here's the unicode sheet for egyptian hieroglyphs. NSFW, but only for the dick. 130B 8-A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …is that a thing that exists?

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST COFFER TOAST
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the nice comment! We appreciate your taking the time to give us a read. :3

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST COFFER TOAST
item.content:encoded: I wasn’t much help but I still feel honored to know I gave eyes to it before it was posted, even if only a few minutes before! I said it before and I’ll say it again, you guys did a great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: inb4 somebody posts the unicode character for the dick hieroglyph

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST COFFER TOAST
item.content:encoded: εїз ♡

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST COFFER TOAST
item.content:encoded: Daww I feel so important, happy to help!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHAT'S YER OFFER?

item.title: AUTHOR POST COFFER TOAST
item.content:encoded:
So here is very important research information I read yes: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Osiris_myth

Also, image source is here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Phallus_(hieroglyph) Yes, this exists. Yes, you can see this in real life. Wild.

Big, big thanks to all of our chat reviewers! You are much appreciated and loved.

Thank you for Snappy in chat for providing the name for the article. Cheers!

I had a great time writing this—can't thank my co-author lvdoomien enough for being patient with my crazy schedule and meeting up to discuss and work on the draft over the last couple months. This has been in progress since December! lvdoomien had a lot of great ideas for the addenda content in particular, and I really enjoyed the back-and-forth regarding the wacky niche Ancient Egyptian mythology stories we discussed.

This is a sort of different feel on a standard SCP article. I was hoping we could convey just absolute flabbergast-ment on the part of the researchers who had to handle this anomaly, and crazy things happening faster than they could clinically record them. Because when the Sons of Horus are vandalizing your parking lot and birds are pooping all over your communication tower, who has time to write a perfectly clinical SCP document?

To all readers, I hope you enjoyed this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you include a Courage the Cowardly Dog reference in your SCP?

item.title: Thank you again
item.content:encoded:
Okay let me write this up again because oh my God.

I want to thank Zyn the most for the opportunity to try this one again. I'll always appreciate how kind she is to me and how we were able to come up with an SCP we're both happy with.

I want to thank RockTeethMothEyes and cybersqyd for the greenlights to my original idea so that I can finally try to make an SCP (and fail LMAO).

Thank you to OriCat for providing help with my original draft, I'm sorry you weren't able to help much on this iteration but I appreciate you regardless.

Thank you to Geka-Gaku, DianaBerry, Mew-ltwoverse, Calibri_Bold, Simartar, and Snappy for liking the draft tonight when Zyn and I were flailing it around.

Thanks to uhh everyone in genchat that is super cool and chill I appreciate yall.

And thanks to AbsentmindedNihilist for inspiring me with SCP-3807 and your goofy Egyptian mythos shenanigans. Sorry for no crosslink this iteration ;-; it didn't mesh with the new idea as well as it did before but I'm still grateful that you'd let me do it the first time.
